rescue_reasoning: Although the verbatim sub-text does not mention climate change/adaptation explicitly (it discusses surveillance and awareness: “Conduct surveillance of mosquitoes… ensure an inspection system for pathogens” and “conducting surveillance of fire ants, and raising awareness”), the parent statement explicitly places these actions under climate-related health adaptation: “TMG will strengthen health adaptation… to minimize heatstroke, infectious disease risks, and air-pollution health hazards.” Given climate_screen = explicit_parent, the gate was too strict; retain as Adaptation (environmental health surveillance to reduce infectious disease/invasive species risks).
policy_statement: TMG will reduce mosquito-borne infectious disease risk by providing occurrence/prevention/source-control information, conducting mosquito surveillance, and ensuring pathogen inspection systems; it will also surveil fire ants with partners and raise awareness to reduce risks from harmful alien species.
mechanism_description: TMG provides prevention and source-control information, conducts mosquito and fire ant surveillance, and ensures pathogen inspection systems to reduce risks from mosquito-borne infectious diseases and harmful alien species.
primary_causal_pathway: Climate-sensitive biological hazard → early detection + prevention/control → ↓ exposure/vulnerability → ↓ climate impacts (health/ecosystem/economic)
causal_mechanism_detail:
1) Change in human activity/system: Public agencies expand environmental health surveillance, pathogen/vector inspection, and public guidance (prevention and source-control behaviors), plus interagency monitoring of invasive species.  
2) Mediating physical/ecological process: Earlier detection and faster response reduces vector populations, interrupts pathogen transmission chains, and limits establishment/spread of invasive species through inspection, containment, and eradication actions.  
3) Climate-relevant outcome: Reduced incidence/severity of climate-sensitive infectious diseases and reduced damages from invasive species as warming and altered precipitation expand habitat suitability and transmission seasons.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is lowering climate-driven health/ecosystem damages by detecting, preventing, and controlling mosquito-borne disease outbreaks and invasive species incursions.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: Climate-sensitive hazards (vectors/pathogens/invasive species) are managed via surveillance/inspection and prevention to reduce exposure and vulnerability, which is an adaptation pathway.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While there may be minor mitigation co-benefits (e.g., avoided emergency operations) or resource-efficiency aspects, the intended and largest effect is protecting health and ecosystems from climate-amplified biological risks.  
3) Edge cases applied: If surveillance is primarily for general public health independent of climate, it is still adaptation when framed around climate-sensitive risk (mosquito-borne disease expansion, invasive species spread). If a policy’s dominant aim were biodiversity conservation/carbon sinks, it could shift toward Nature-Based Solutions, but this mechanism is surveillance/awareness/inspection, not ecosystem enhancement.  
4) Confirm primary applies to ALL instances: Any policy that uses environmental health surveillance + awareness + inspection to reduce infectious disease or invasive species risk functions chiefly by reducing vulnerability to climate-sensitive biological hazards, so Adaptation remains the primary category.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment deferred: Any further sub-classification (e.g., “public health adaptation,” “biosecurity,” “early warning systems”) depends on policy-specific evidence and is deferred to policy-level assessment.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: Could be misclassified if a specific instance is purely routine biosecurity with no climate linkage; also, invasive-species actions that materially enhance ecosystems/carbon storage (restoration-based) would belong under Nature-Based Solutions, but surveillance/awareness/inspection alone remains Adaptation.
key_indicators: "providing information"; "Conduct surveillance of mosquitoes"; "ensure an inspection system for pathogens"; "conducting surveillance of fire ants"; "raising awareness of Tokyo residents"; "Reduce the risk of infectious disease outbreak"
co_benefits: Improved routine public health capacity and non-climate outbreak preparedness; reduced nuisance biting and improved quality of life; broader biodiversity/eco-protection from invasive species beyond climate drivers.
instance_edge_case_notes: Classifiable as either a public program (often mapped to an enabling/information or planning approach) or a direct operational intervention; treated here as a programmatic instrument because it specifies implementation actions (surveillance + inspection + awareness) rather than a binding mandate/standard. No explicit incentives are mentioned, but “program” category best matches the bundle of ongoing government services.
